behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing, and missing fields are all reported clearly.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a unit test of only the validator can miss orchestration bugs where downstream modules still run. REAL-INFRA: no external OS/network needed. Minimum failure boundary is local pipeline handoff (intake handler -> validator -> downstream gates).
behaviors.test_description: Run intake through the real local intake pipeline with a payload missing `name` and `insurance_id`; assert response lists both missing fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling modules are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date-of-birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in deterministic date parsing/range logic inside one function/object. No module handoff or external dependency required.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test DOB validator with table-driven inputs: future date, >150 years old date, invalid string, and valid boundary dates; assert exact validation outcomes.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code and interprets eligible/not-eligible/pending responses.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correctness includes real HTTP request shape and response handling against payer API contract. MOCK-HIDE: mocked client can hide serialization/header/query mismatches that only appear over real HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Call the insurance client against a real payer sandbox (or local HTTP contract server over actual HTTP) and verify outbound fields (`patient_id`, `plan_code`) plus mapping of `eligible`, `not eligible`, and `pending` responses into internal status.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: needs real network failure mode (timeout/connection refused/DNS) from HTTP stack. MOCK-HIDE: raising a mocked exception may not match real client behavior/retry/timeout paths.
behaviors.test_description: Execute intake with insurance endpoint pointed to an unreachable host or blocked port; assert eligibility becomes `pending` and pipeline continues to triage/scheduling/confirmation instead of failing closed.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to emergency symptom combinations.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is medical urgency classification quality from a real AI model. MOCK-MISS: deterministic stubs cannot catch misclassification by the real model.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage against the real medical reasoning model for emergency symptom sets (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) across repeated trials; assert high-urgency classification meets acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural-language explanation, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: whether explanation is meaningful/clinically reviewable depends on real model generation quality and variability. Mocked text generation can trivially pass while real outputs fail quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke real triage model and evaluate explanation outputs against rubric checks (contains rationale tied to symptoms, coherent language, not empty/template-only) over multiple runs.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high: same-day, medium: within 48h).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure typically appears in local module handoff between triage result, scheduling logic, and calendar DB state. No real external API required.
behaviors.test_description: With a seeded local calendar DB, run scheduling service end-to-end for high and medium urgency cases; assert chosen slot is the earliest valid slot and respects same-day/48-hour constraints.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: race conditions are missed by single-function tests and naive mocks. Needs real local DB locking/transaction semantics under concurrent writers; still local infra.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests against the same local calendar DB slot and assert only one booking succeeds (or one is re-routed), with no duplicate appointment rows for identical time/provider.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-appropriate prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this depends on composing data from scheduling/visit-type modules into message rendering; a pure template unit test can miss missing-field wiring defects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute confirm step with real local data flow from scheduled appointment + visit type mapping; assert message contains required fields and correct prep instructions for each visit type.

behaviors.description: Full intake pipeline (validate -> insurance -> triage -> schedule -> confirm) completes automatically for a real submission.
behaviors.justification: Covers end-to-end orchestration plus one real AI decision step. LLM-DEP: completion robustness can fail on real model output variability/format deviations; mocks can hide this. MULTI-STEP agentic chain is not required, so Workflow is plausible but not minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real end-to-end intake submission in a test environment using real payer sandbox/API path, real triage model call, and real calendar DB; assert pipeline reaches confirmation without manual intervention.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Includes real model call, so it truly requires Agentic rather than only System.
